flashcards.q: Define natural vegetation.
flashcards.a: Plant community that has grown naturally without human aid and has been left undisturbed for a long time; also called virgin vegetation.

flashcards.q: What is endemic or indigenous species?
flashcards.a: Plant or animal species that are purely Indian, found nowhere else in the world.

flashcards.q: What is exotic species?
flashcards.a: Plant or animal species that have come from outside India and been introduced into the country.

flashcards.q: Define flora.
flashcards.a: Refers to plants of a particular region or period.

flashcards.q: Define fauna.
flashcards.a: Refers to animal species of a particular region or period.

flashcards.q: How many plant species does India have?
flashcards.a: Approximately 47,000 plant species.

flashcards.q: How many flowering plants does India have?
flashcards.a: Approximately 15,000 flowering plants (6% of world's total).

flashcards.q: What is India's rank globally in plant diversity?
flashcards.a: Tenth place globally and fourth place in Asia.

flashcards.q: How many animal species does India have?
flashcards.a: Approximately 90,000 animal species.

flashcards.q: How many bird species does India have?
flashcards.a: About 2,000 bird species (13% of world's total).

flashcards.q: How many fish species does India have?
flashcards.a: 2,546 fish species (nearly 12% of world's stock).

flashcards.q: What is India's position as a bio-diversity country?
flashcards.a: One of the 12 mega bio-diversity countries of the world.

flashcards.q: Name the five major vegetation types of India.
flashcards.a: Tropical Evergreen Forests, Tropical Deciduous Forests, Tropical Thorn Forests and Scrubs, Montane Forests, and Mangrove Forests.

flashcards.q: Where are Tropical Evergreen Forests found?
flashcards.a: Western Ghats, Lakshadweep, Andaman-Nicobar Islands, upper Assam, and Tamil Nadu coast.

flashcards.q: What is the rainfall requirement for Tropical Evergreen Forests?
flashcards.a: More than 200 cm of rainfall annually.

flashcards.q: What is special about Tropical Evergreen Forests?
flashcards.a: Trees remain green year-round with no definite leaf-shedding season due to warm, wet climate throughout the year.

flashcards.q: What is the maximum height of trees in Tropical Evergreen Forests?
flashcards.a: Up to 60 metres or more.

flashcards.q: Name commercially important trees of Tropical Evergreen Forests.
flashcards.a: Ebony, mahogany, rosewood, rubber, and cinchona.

flashcards.q: What animals are found in Tropical Evergreen Forests?
flashcards.a: Elephants, monkeys, lemurs, deer, one-horned rhinoceroses, birds, bats, sloths, scorpions, and snails.

flashcards.q: Why are Tropical Deciduous Forests called monsoon forests?
flashcards.a: They are dependent on monsoon rainfall of 70-200 cm annually for their survival.

flashcards.q: What is the rainfall range for Tropical Deciduous Forests?
flashcards.a: Between 70 cm and 200 cm annually.

flashcards.q: How long do trees in Tropical Deciduous Forests shed their leaves?
flashcards.a: For about 6 to 8 weeks in the dry summer season.

flashcards.q: What is the most widespread forest type in India?
flashcards.a: Tropical Deciduous Forests.

flashcards.q: What are Moist Deciduous Forests?
flashcards.a: Deciduous forests receiving 100-200 cm rainfall, found in northeastern states, foothills, and eastern slopes of Western Ghats.

flashcards.q: What is the dominant tree species in Moist Deciduous Forests?
flashcards.a: Teak.

flashcards.q: Name other commercially important trees in Moist Deciduous Forests.
flashcards.a: Bamboos, sal, shisham, sandalwood, khair, kusum, arjun, and mulberry.

flashcards.q: What are Dry Deciduous Forests?
flashcards.a: Deciduous forests receiving 70-100 cm rainfall, found in peninsular plateau and northern plains.

flashcards.q: What animals are found in Dry Deciduous Forests?
flashcards.a: Lions, tigers, pigs, deer, elephants, birds, lizards, snakes, and tortoises.

flashcards.q: What is the rainfall requirement for Tropical Thorn Forests?
flashcards.a: Less than 70 cm annually.

flashcards.q: Where are Tropical Thorn Forests found?
flashcards.a: Northwestern India, including semi-arid areas of Gujarat, Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Uttar Pradesh, and Haryana.

flashcards.q: What are the main plant species in Tropical Thorn Forests?
flashcards.a: Acacias, palms, euphorbias, and cacti.

flashcards.q: What are adaptations of Tropical Thorn Forest plants?
flashcards.a: Long roots penetrating deep soil, succulent stems for water storage, small thick leaves to minimize evaporation.

flashcards.q: What animals are found in Tropical Thorn Forests?
flashcards.a: Rats, mice, rabbits, foxes, wolves, tigers, lions, wild ass, horses, and camels.

flashcards.q: What happens to vegetation with increasing altitude in Montane Forests?
flashcards.a: Temperature decreases causing a succession of vegetation belts from broad-leaf to coniferous to Alpine types.

flashcards.q: What vegetation exists at 1000-2000m altitude in Montane Forests?
flashcards.a: Wet temperate forests with evergreen broad-leaf trees like oaks and chestnuts.

flashcards.q: What vegetation exists at 1500-3000m altitude in Montane Forests?
flashcards.a: Temperate forests with coniferous trees like pine, deodar, silver fir, spruce, and cedar.

flashcards.q: What vegetation exists above 3600m altitude in Montane Forests?
flashcards.a: Alpine vegetation with stunted trees, shrubs, and grasslands.

flashcards.q: What animals are found in Montane Forests?
flashcards.a: Kashmir stag, spotted deer, wild sheep, Tibetan antelope, yak, snow leopard, squirrels, ibex, bears, and red panda.

flashcards.q: What are Mangrove Forests?
flashcards.a: Tidal forests found in coastal areas influenced by tides, with roots submerged in saltwater.

flashcards.q: Where are Mangrove Forests located in India?
flashcards.a: In deltas of Ganga, Mahanadi, Krishna, Godavari, and Kaveri rivers.

flashcards.q: What is the main tree species in Ganga-Brahmaputra Mangrove Forests?
flashcards.a: Sundari tree, which provides durable hard timber.

flashcards.q: What other trees grow in Mangrove Forests?
flashcards.a: Palm, coconut, keora, and agar.

flashcards.q: What is the famous animal of Mangrove Forests?
flashcards.a: Royal Bengal Tiger.

flashcards.q: What other animals are found in Mangrove Forests?
flashcards.a: Turtles, crocodiles, gharials, and snakes.

flashcards.q: How many medicinal plants are described in Ayurveda?
flashcards.a: Approximately 2,000 plants.

flashcards.q: How many medicinal plants are in regular use in India?
flashcards.a: At least 500.

flashcards.q: How many medicinal plants are threatened in India?
flashcards.a: 352 total, with 52 critically threatened and 49 endangered.

flashcards.q: What is Sarpagandha used for?
flashcards.a: Treating blood pressure; found only in India.

flashcards.q: What are the uses of Jamun?
flashcards.a: Juice prevents diabetes; has digestive properties; seed powder controls diabetes.

flashcards.q: What is Arjun used for?
flashcards.a: Fresh leaf juice cures heart problems and regulates blood pressure.

flashcards.q: What is Babool used for?
flashcards.a: Leaves cure eye sores; gum used as a tonic.

flashcards.q: What is Neem used for?
flashcards.a: Has high antibiotic and antibacterial properties.

flashcards.q: What is Tulsi used for?
flashcards.a: Cures cough and cold.

flashcards.q: What is Kachnar used for?
flashcards.a: Cures asthma and ulcers; roots and buds good for digestion.

flashcards.q: Where are elephants found in India?
flashcards.a: In hot wet forests of Assam, Karnataka, and Kerala.

flashcards.q: Where are one-horned rhinoceroses found?
flashcards.a: In swampy and marshy lands of Assam and West Bengal.

flashcards.q: Where is the Indian lion found?
flashcards.a: In the Gir Forest of Gujarat.

flashcards.q: Why is the Gir Forest important?
flashcards.a: It is the last remaining habitat of the Asiatic lion.

flashcards.q: Where are tigers found in India?
flashcards.a: In forests of Madhya Pradesh, Sundarbans of West Bengal, and Himalayan regions.

flashcards.q: Is India the only country with both tigers and lions?
flashcards.a: Yes, India is the only country in the world with both tigers and lions.

flashcards.q: What is gharial?
flashcards.a: A type of crocodile; the only representative of its kind found in the world today.

flashcards.q: What migratory bird comes to India during winter?
flashcards.a: Siberian Crane.

flashcards.q: Where do Siberian Cranes come in India?
flashcards.a: To Indian wetlands like Rann of Kachchh.

flashcards.q: What bird comes to Rann of Kachchh?
flashcards.a: Flamingos with brilliant pink plumage build nest mounds from salty mud.

flashcards.q: How many plant species are endangered in India?
flashcards.a: Approximately 1,300 plant species are endangered.

flashcards.q: How many plant species are extinct in India?
flashcards.a: 20 species are extinct.

flashcards.q: What is the Wildlife Protection Act?
flashcards.a: Implemented in 1972 in India to provide legal framework for wildlife conservation.

flashcards.q: How many biosphere reserves are established in India?
flashcards.a: 18 biosphere reserves.

flashcards.q: How many biosphere reserves are internationally recognized?
flashcards.a: 12 out of 18 are included in the world network of biosphere reserves.

flashcards.q: Name some internationally recognized biosphere reserves.
flashcards.a: Sundarbans, Nanda Devi, Gulf of Mannar, Nilgiri, Nokrek, Great Nicobar, Simlipal, Pachmarhi, Achanakmar-Amarkantak, Agasthyamalai, Kangchendzonga, Panna.

flashcards.q: How many National Parks are established in India?
flashcards.a: 106 National Parks.

flashcards.q: How many Wildlife Sanctuaries are established in India?
flashcards.a: 573 Wildlife Sanctuaries.

flashcards.q: What is Project Tiger?
flashcards.a: A conservation project initiated to protect endangered tigers and their habitats.

flashcards.q: What is Project Rhino?
flashcards.a: A conservation project focused on protecting endangered rhinoceros populations.

flashcards.q: What is Project Great Indian Bustard?
flashcards.a: A conservation project aimed at protecting the endangered Great Indian Bustard bird.

flashcards.q: What is Dorothy Hodgkin known for?
flashcards.a: Nobel Prize-winning chemist (1964) who studied the structure of Vitamin B12.

flashcards.q: What is the role of insects in ecosystems?
flashcards.a: Many insects help in pollination of crops and fruit trees and exert biological control on harmful insects.

flashcards.q: What is the significance of biodiversity for agriculture?
flashcards.a: Crops were selected from bio-diverse environments and wild crop relatives contain genes for disease resistance.

flashcards.q: What animals were selected from wildlife?
flashcards.a: Milch animals (dairy cattle), draught animals (oxen), transportation animals, and sources of meat and eggs.

flashcards.q: What is the importance of fish?
flashcards.a: Fish provide nutritive food and support livelihoods through fishing.

flashcards.q: What causes threats to biodiversity?
flashcards.a: Hunting for commercial purposes, pollution from industrial waste, acid deposits, introduction of alien species, and deforestation.

flashcards.q: What is a biosphere reserve?
flashcards.a: Protected area established to conserve biodiversity by protecting flora and fauna in natural habitats.
